content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual-Contrastive Prompting: Reducing LLM Hallucination via Factually Anchored Contrasts",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) generate factually incorrect or hallucinated outputs, especially in open-domain question answering tasks. Current prompting and retrieval-augmented methods do not adequately constrain generations to verifiable knowledge.",
  "motivation": "LLMs are increasingly used in knowledge-intensive domains where factual reliability is critical, yet existing methods lack mechanisms to distinguish true from plausible-but-false generations. By integrating contrastive factual grounding at the prompting stage, we can more explicitly steer LLMs toward factual fidelity. This is timely given the growing demand for trustworthy AI in high-stakes applications.",
  "method": "We propose Counterfactual-Contrastive Prompting (CCP): for each input query, the prompt is augmented with both the question and a set of carefully constructed counterfactual statements that are similar in structure but factually false, alongside a gold fact statement. The LLM is instructed to (1) answer the original question and (2) explicitly identify which statements are factual and which are not, providing justifications grounded in external evidence (when available). This contrastive setup encourages the model to learn robust factual discrimination at generation time, rather than relying purely on internal heuristics or post-hoc retrieval.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate CCP on open-domain QA datasets (e.g., Natural Questions, TriviaQA) and medical fact-checking corpora. Baselines will include standard few-shot prompting, retrieval-augmented generation, and citation-based prompt methods. Metrics will track factual accuracy, hallucination rate, and justification quality, with human and automated assessments."
}
